blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلسَّمَآءِ ذَاتِ ٱلْبُرُوجِ (۱)
By the heaven with its Houses, (1)
وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْمَوْعُودِ (۲)
by the Promised Day, (2)
وَشَاهِدٍ وَمَشْهُودٍ (۳)
by the Witness and the Witnessed: (3)
قُتِلَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْأُخْدُودِ (٤)
perish the People of the Ditch! (4)
ٱلنَّارِ ذَاتِ ٱلْوَقُودِ (٥)
The fire abounding in fuel, (5)
إِذْ هُمْ عَلَيْهَا قُعُودٌ (٦)
above which they sat (6)
وَهُمْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَفْعَلُونَ بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ شُهُودٌ (۷)
as they were themselves witnesses to what they did to the faithful. (7)
وَمَا نَقَمُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ إِلَّآ أَن يُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَمِيدِ (۸)
They were vindictive towards them only because they had faith in Allah, the All-mighty and the All-laudable, (8)
ٱلَّذِى لَهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدٌ (۹)
to whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is witness to all things. (9)

Key Points

“Burūj” (بروج) is the plural of “Burj” (برج), meaning something apparent and manifest, just as “tabarruj” (تبرّج) means the display of adornment. If tall and elevated buildings are called burj, it is because they are more apparent among other structures. The intent of “Burūj” in these verses is the stars of the sky, which, like a high tower, are apparent and shining from afar. As He says in another verse: «و لقد جعلنا فى السماء بروجاً و زیّناها للناظرین» (28)

Approximately thirty interpretations have been given for the verse “Shāhid wa Mashhūd” (witness and witnessed), including that the Shāhid (witness) is Allah and the Mashhūd (witnessed) is everything other than Allah (mā siwā Allāh), meaning, an oath by the Creator and by the entire existence. Imam Hassan عليه السلام says (29): The intent of the Shāhid (witness) is the Prophet, based on the verse «انّا ارسلناك شاهداً...» (30) and the intent of the Mashhūd (witnessed) is the Day of Judgment, based on the verse «ذلك یوم مجموع له الناس ذلك یوم مشهود» (31)

“Waqūd” (وقود) means kindling or fuel. “Ukhdūd” (اُخدود) means a deep trench or pit. The intent of “Aṣḥāb al-Ukhdūd” (Companions of the Trench) are those who created a wide trench in the earth and kindled a great fire in it, and then threw the believers into the fire, burning them alive. Just as they did in the case of Prophet Abraham. However, various narrations have been reported regarding who these oppressors and those believers were, but they are not mentioned in the Quran.

28) Hijr, 16. 29) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 30) Al-Ahzab, 45. 31) Hud, 103.

Messages

1- All existence holds value before Allah and is worthy of an oath, including the sky and its stars. «و السماء ذات البروج»

2- Do not be heedless of the Day of Judgment, for it is the appointed meeting place for all. «الیوم الموعود»

3- You and your deeds are all under observation, specifically under the observation of God. «و شاهد و مشهود»

4- Declaring disassociation (barā’ah) and expressing hatred towards the oppressor is necessary. «قتل اصحاب الاخدود»

5- In recounting history, the lessons (cibrah) are important, not the details. (No mention is made of the location of the Ukhdūd, the time of the incident, the names or number of the oppressors, or the number of martyrs, because these details do not affect the lesson learned.) «قتل اصحاب الاخدود»

6- The mindset of fighting oppression and crushing the tyrant is a virtue, even if the tyrant no longer exists. (The Companions of the Trench are not present today, just as Abu Lahab is not present today; the spirit of hatred towards them must remain alive. «تبّت یدا ابى لهب و تبّ») «قتل اصحاب الاخدود»

7- Burning is the severest type of torment, and among the various types of killing, Allah specifically mentioned the throwing of believers into the fire. «النار ذات الوقود»

8- Recounting acts of oppression and victimhood, and mentioning calamities (maṣībah) and reciting eulogies (rawḍah khwānī), has a Quranic precedent. «اصحاب الاخدود النار ذات الوقود اذ هم علیها قعود...»

9- Committing a sin is one issue, but hard-heartedness, spectating, and consenting to it is another issue. «و هم على ما یفعلون بالمؤمنین شهود»

10- The oppressors who witness the torture of the believers should know that God is a Witness over them and their actions. «شاهد و مشهود - هم على ما یفعلون بالمؤمنین شهود»

11- Steadfastness in faith has a price. «و ما نقموا منهم الاّ ان یؤمنوا» (The phrase «یؤمنوا» [present tense] instead of «آمنوا» [past tense] is a sign that the disbelievers were upset by the believers' persistence, and if they had abandoned their faith, there would have been no torture.)

12- The logic of the disbeliever is threat and vengeance. «و ما نقموا منهم»

13- In the view of the disbelievers, faith is the greatest crime, and they will not be satisfied with anything less than the abandonment of faith. «و ما نقموا منهم الاّ أن یؤمنوا...»

14- The disbelievers should know that the Protector of the believers is the Mighty God (Allāh al-‘Azīz), Who has the power to take vengeance. «یؤمنوا باللّه العزیز»

15- The attraction of connection to the Capable God resolves the difficulties associated with martyrdom and self-sacrifice in the way of God. «یؤمنوا باللّه العزیز الحمید»

16- True sovereignty belongs to Allah. «له ملك السموات و الارض»

17- Divine sovereignty is accompanied by His knowledge and presence. «واللّه على كُلّ شى ءٍ شهید»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Burūj

This Surah has twenty-two verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse, and it refers to the shining stars that glitter in the sky like a high tower (burj).

The Surah begins by describing the crimes of a group of torturers who dug a deep trench and kindled a massive fire in it, threatening the believers with burning, and throwing anyone who did not abandon their faith into the fire. Allah promises them the severe fire of Hell, which is incomparable to the fire of this world, thereby comforting the believers and inviting them to resist the oppressors.

The continuation of the Surah briefly refers to the story of Pharaoh and the people of Thamūd, showing how, despite all their power, they were defeated by the Divine Will and perished. The concluding verses of the Surah express the greatness of the Quran and the high status of Divine Revelation.

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ فَتَنُوا۟ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَتُوبُوا۟ فَلَهُمْ عَذَابُ جَهَنَّمَ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابُ ٱلْحَرِيقِ (۱٠)
Indeed those who persecute the faithful, men and women, and do not repent thereafter, there is the punishment of hell for them and the punishment of burning. (10)
إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٌ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْكَبِيرُ (۱۱)
Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds—for them will be gardens with streams running in them. That is the supreme success. (11)

Key Points

The word fitnah (فتنة) originally means placing gold in fire to determine its purity. In technical usage, it is also used to mean torment ('adhab), torture, or severe trials. However, in the current Ayah under discussion, it means torment and affliction. (32) Just as He says in another Ayah: «یوم هم على النار یفتنون ذوقوا فتنتكم هذا الّذى كنتم به تستعجلون» (33)

Throughout the bitter and difficult events of history, believing men and women have always stood side-by-side, and the role of women in reformative and revolutionary movements is unforgettable. Therefore, the criminal oppressors subjected them to torment and torture just like the men. «فتنوا المؤمنین و المؤمنات»

32) Tafsir Namuneh. 33) Adh-Dhariyat, 13 and 14.

Messages

1- The People of the Ditch (Ashab al-Ukhdud) and all torturers throughout history are subject to Divine wrath. «الّذین فَتنوا المؤمنین...»

2- Believing women, alongside men, endured torment and torture willingly. «فَتنوا المؤمنین و المؤمنات»

3- In defending the oppressed, there is no difference between men and women. «فَتنوا المؤمنین و المؤمنات ...»

4- The path of repentance is always open, and there is no dead end for anyone; therefore, despairing of Allah’s mercy is forbidden. (Yes, Allah invited even the torturers who burned the believers to repent.) «فتنوا... ثمّ لم یتوبوا»

5- Allah accepts repentance, even if a long time has passed. «ثُمّ»

6- The recompense for burning is burning. «النّار ذات الوقود - عذاب الحریق»

7- Glad tidings (Bisharah) and warning (Indhar) must accompany one another. «عذاب الحریق... جنات تجرى...»

8- The best pleasures are those derived from nature. «جنّات... اَنهار»

9- For the torturing disbelievers, there are two torments: the torment of disbelief and the torment of torture. «فلهم عذاب جهنّم و لهم عذاب الحریق»

10- This world is fleeting. Both the enjoyments of the oppressors and the hardships of the believers are temporary. Be mindful of the Hereafter, for the torment of the dwellers of Hell is endless, and the reward of the dwellers of Paradise is eternal. «فتنوا المؤمنین... لهم عذاب الحریق - الّذین آمنوا... لهم جنّات»

Ayahs

إِنَّ بَطْشَ رَبِّكَ لَشَدِيدٌ (۱۲)
Indeed your Lord’s striking is severe. (12)
إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ يُبْدِئُ وَيُعِيدُ (۱۳)
It is indeed He who originates and brings back, (13)
وَهُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلْوَدُودُ (۱٤)
and He is the All-forgiving, the All-affectionate, (14)
ذُو ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱلْمَجِيدُ (۱٥)
Lord of the Throne, the All-glorious, (15)
فَعَّالٌ لِّمَا يُرِيدُ (۱٦)
doer of what He desires. (16)

Key Points

"Baṭsh" (بَطش) means seizing accompanied by wrath and dominance. "ʿArsh" (عَرش) means the throne of sovereignty and is a metaphor for Divine administration. The ʿArsh has also been interpreted as the station that encompasses all existence. Therefore, "Dhul-ʿArsh" (ذوالعرش) means the Owner of all existence and the center for the administration of existence.

Messages

1- Divine wrath and dominance are such that no one and nothing can prevent them. «انّ بطش ربّك لشدید»

2- Wrath and punishment are among the affairs of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and serve to advance the goals of the mission and console the Prophet. «بطش ربّك»

3- Creating and restoring (resurrecting) is the constant work of Allah. «انّه هو یبدئ و یعید»

4- Allah's power over creating and restoring is equal. «یبدئ و یعید»

5- Allah possesses both wrath and dominance, and forgiveness and affection. «بطش ربّك لشدید... و هو الغفور الودود»

6- In addition to creation, the administration and sovereignty over all things belong to Him. «ذوالعرش»

7- In wrath and in mercy, nothing prevents the Divine Will. «بطش ربّك لشدید - هو الغفور الودود - فعّال لما یرید»

8- Divine actions have no limitations. «لما یُرید» (It is self-evident that the Divine Will is accompanied by wisdom, and He does not intend to do anything that is not wise.)

Ayahs

هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ٱلْجُنُودِ (۱۷)
Did you receive the story of the hosts (17)
فِرْعَوْنَ وَثَمُودَ (۱۸)
of Pharaoh and Thamud? (18)
بَلِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فِى تَكْذِيبٍ (۱۹)
Indeed the faithless dwell in denial, (19)
وَٱللَّهُ مِن وَرَآئِهِم مُّحِيطٌۢ (۲٠)
and Allah besieges them from all around. (20)
بَلْ هُوَ قُرْءَانٌ مَّجِيدٌ (۲۱)
Indeed it is a glorious Quran, (21)
فِى لَوْحٍ مَّحْفُوظٍۭ (۲۲)
in a preserved tablet. (22)

Key Points

The peoples of Pharaoh and Thamūd were among the most powerful nations in terms of strength, civilization, and resources. Their power is mentioned in other verses of the Quran. Therefore, these two nations are named here. Declaring Allah's ability to crush and destroy superpowers is a form of reassurance for the believers. Whenever the word "Quran" (قرآن) appears with the definite article (alif and lām), it refers to the Book of the Quran. However, without the definite article, it means maqru' (مقروء), meaning "that which is read." As He says in these verses: بل هو قرآن مجید (Nay, but it is a glorious Quran), meaning the words of revelation have been recited to the Prophet.

Messages

1- First, make the listener eager, then deliver your speech. هل اتاك حدیث الجنود

2- One must be aware of the history of past nations and take lessons from Allah's dealings with them. هل اتاك حدیث الجنود فرعون و ثمود

3- The most equipped armies and forces are insignificant before His wrath. الجنود فرعون و ثمود

4- The disbelievers insist and persist in denying the Quran and its promises. بل الّذین كفروا فى تكذیب

5- Oppressors are not outside the sphere of Divine power and have no means of escape, yet they do not understand this themselves. و اللّه من ورائهم محیط (The word warā'ihim [ورائهم] indicates their heedlessness regarding Allah's knowledge and power.)

6- Both Allah is the Possessor of glory and majesty, and His Quran is glorious. ذو العرش المجید... بل هو قرآن مجید

7- The Tablet upon which the Quran is recorded is unknown to us, although it is preserved with Allah. فى لوح محفوظ

8- The Quran is safeguarded against every kind of threat and distortion. قرآن مجید فى لوح محفوظ
